job.d: work that you do to earn money
job.e: My dad has a job at the store.

june.d: The sixth month of the year

july.d: The seventh month of the year
july.e: My birthday is in July, so I always have fun that month.

james.e: My friend James is coming over to play today.

january.d: The first month of the year

justice.d: Making sure things are fair and right
justice.e: The police work to make sure there is justice in the town.

jobs.d: Work that you get paid to do
jobs.e: Many people have different jobs in our town.

join.d: To become a part of a group or activity
join.e: The new student decided to join the soccer team.

judge.d: A person who decides if someone is right or wrong
judge.e: The judge listened to both sides before making a decision.

japan.d: a country in Asia with a rich culture
japan.e: My aunt visited Japan and brought back beautiful art.

jack.d: A tool used to lift heavy things like cars
jack.e: The mechanic used a jack to lift the car.

japanese.e: The family liked to eat Japanese food.

joined.d: To come together and become one.
joined.e: The two pieces of the puzzle joined together.

joint.d: The place where two bones in your body connect.
joint.e: My knee joint hurts when I run.

journal.d: A book where you write down your thoughts and feelings.
journal.e: I write in my journal every night before bed.

joe.e: My friend Joe likes to play soccer.

jones.d: A common last name for a person
jones.e: My friend Jones is coming over to play.

joke.d: something funny that people say to make others laugh
joke.e: The comedian told a really funny joke.

jewish.d: Relating to the religion and culture of the Jewish people
jewish.e: The Jewish holiday of Hanukkah is celebrated every year.

journey.d: A trip or travel from one place to another
journey.e: The family went on a long journey to visit their grandparents.

jump.d: To push off the ground and move up or forward through the air
jump.e: The frog jumped high to catch the bug.

jews.d: People who believe in the Jewish religion
jews.e: Many Jews celebrate special holidays like Hanukkah

jim.e: My friend Jim likes to play basketball with me

jersey.d: A type of shirt, often worn for sports
jersey.e: My favorite soccer jersey has the team's logo on it.

jail.d: A place where people are kept if they break the law.
jail.e: The police took the thief to jail.

jean.d: A type of sturdy pants made from a special material.
jean.e: My favorite jeans have rips in the knees.

joy.d: A feeling of being very happy.
joy.e: Her new puppy brought her so much joy.

jackson.e: My friend Jackson likes to play outside.

joseph.e: Joseph helped his mom bake cookies for the school bake sale.

jordan.e: Jordan is the name of my best friend.

junior.d: Someone who is younger or lower in rank.
junior.e: The junior player on the team was just starting out.

judges.d: people who make decisions in a court of law
judges.e: The judges listened carefully to the court case.
